seoTitle: Orange Springs vs Ord River — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Orange Springs and Ord River. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Orange Springs vs Ord River: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Orange Springs and Ord River.
heading: Orange Springs vs Ord River — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Orange Springs, Western Australia and Ord River, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Orange Springs has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Ord River (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Orange Springs or Ord River safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Orange Springs has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Ord River scores 60/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Orange Springs scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Ord River scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Orange Springs or Ord River?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Orange Springs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
